get.description: Get health information about the LiteAI server.
Subscribe to global events from the LiteAI system using server-sent events.
Retrieve the current global LiteAI configuration settings and preferences.

patch.description: Update global LiteAI configuration settings and preferences.

post.description: Clean up and dispose all LiteAI instances, releasing all resources.
Open a native OS folder picker dialog and return the selected path.

200.description: Selected folder path or null if cancelled

get.description: Read and merge all channel and main log files into a single sorted stream.

200.description: Combined log lines sorted by timestamp, plus discovered services

post.description: Write a log entry to the server logs with specified level and metadata.

service.description: Service name for the log entry

extra.description: Additional metadata for the log entry

get.description: Retrieve global path information for the LiteAI installation (home, state, config).
Retrieve the current telemetry enabled/disabled status. Telemetry is enabled by default; clients can opt out via PATCH /telemetry.

source.description: Where the setting was read from

patch.description: Enable or disable telemetry. The setting is persisted to the global config file and takes effect immediately. Note: if LITEAI_TELEMETRY_DISABLED is set as an environment variable, it takes precedence over this setting.

enabled.description: Set to true to enable telemetry, false to disable (opt-out)

get.description: List files and directories in a specified path on the host system.
Search for files or directories by name pattern on the host system.
Get a list of all available AI providers, including both available and connected ones.
Retrieve available authentication methods for all AI providers.

post.description: Initiate OAuth authorization for a specific AI provider to get an authorization URL.
Handle the OAuth callback from a provider after user authorization.

get.description: Get a list of projects that have been opened with LiteAI.

post.description: Register a project for a given directory. Idempotent — returns the existing project if already registered.

get.description: Retrieve a project by its ID.

patch.description: Update project properties such as name, icon, and commands.

start.description: Startup script to run when creating a new workspace (worktree)

patch.description: Archive a project to hide it from the project list. Data and sessions are preserved.
Restore an archived project so it appears in the project list again.

post.description: Create a git repository for a project directory and return the refreshed project info. Requires a directory parameter.

get.description: Get a list of all active pseudo-terminal (PTY) sessions managed by LiteAI.

post.description: Create a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session for running shell commands and processes.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific pseudo-terminal (PTY) session.

put.description: Update properties of an existing pseudo-terminal (PTY) session.

delete.description: Remove and terminate a specific pseudo-terminal (PTY) session.

get.description: Establish a WebSocket connection to interact with a pseudo-terminal (PTY) session in real-time.
Retrieve the current LiteAI configuration settings and preferences.

patch.description: Update LiteAI configuration settings and preferences.

get.description: Get a list of all configured AI providers and their default models.

post.description: Create a workspace for the current project.

get.description: List all workspaces.

post.description: Create a new git worktree for the current project and run any configured startup scripts.

get.description: List all sandbox worktrees for the current project.

delete.description: Remove a git worktree and delete its branch.

post.description: Reset a worktree branch to the primary default branch.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Filter sessions updated on or after this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

get.description: Get a list of all LiteAI sessions, sorted by most recently updated.

post.description: Create a new LiteAI session for interacting with AI assistants and managing conversations.

get.description: Retrieve the current status of all sessions, including active, idle, and completed states.
Retrieve detailed information about a specific LiteAI session.

delete.description: Delete a session and permanently remove all associated data, including messages and history.

patch.description: Update properties of an existing session, such as title or other metadata.

get.description: Retrieve all child sessions that were forked from the specified parent session.
Retrieve the todo list associated with a specific session, showing tasks and action items.

post.description: Analyze the current application and create an AGENTS.md file with project-specific agent configurations.
Create a new session by forking an existing session at a specific message point.
Abort an active session and stop any ongoing AI processing or command execution.
Create a shareable link for a session, allowing others to view the conversation.

delete.description: Remove the shareable link for a session, making it private again.

get.description: Get the file changes (diff) that resulted from a specific user message in the session.

post.description: Generate a concise summary of the session using AI compaction to preserve key information.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return

get.description: Retrieve all messages in a session, including user prompts and AI responses.

post.description: Create and send a new message to a session, streaming the AI response.

get.description: Retrieve a specific message from a session by its message ID.

delete.description: Permanently delete a specific message (and all of its parts) from a session. This does not revert any file changes that may have been made while processing the message.
Delete a part from a message

patch.description: Update a part in a message

post.description: Create and send a new message to a session asynchronously, starting the session if needed and returning immediately.
Send a new command to a session for execution by the AI assistant.
Execute a shell command within the session context and return the AI's response.
Revert a specific message in a session, undoing its effects and restoring the previous state.
Restore all previously reverted messages in a session.
Approve or deny a permission request from the AI assistant.

get.description: Get all pending permission requests across all sessions.
Get all pending question requests across all sessions.

post.description: Provide answers to a question request from the AI assistant.

answers.description: User answers in order of questions (each answer is an array of selected labels)

post.description: Reject a question request from the AI assistant.

get.description: Search for text patterns across files in the project using ripgrep.
Search for files or directories by name or pattern in the project directory.
Search for workspace symbols like functions, classes, and variables using LSP.
List files and directories in a specified path.
Read the content of a specified file.
Get the git status of all files in the project.
Get the status of all Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.

post.description: Dynamically add a new Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to the system.

get.description: Get tool names grouped by connected MCP server.

post.description: Start OAuth authentication flow for a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.

authorizationUrl.description: URL to open in browser for authorization

delete.description: Remove OAuth credentials for an MCP server

post.description: Complete OAuth authentication for a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server using the authorization code.
Start OAuth flow and wait for callback (opens browser)
Connect an MCP server
Disconnect an MCP server

get.description: Get all available MCP resources from connected servers. Optionally filter by name.

post.summary: Install a plugin from a marketplace

get.description: Get a list of all available build-in tools IDs.
Get a list of available built-in tools with their JSON schema parameters for a specific provider and model combination.
Retrieve instance-scoped information including directory, worktree, and project details.
Retrieve the currently active project that LiteAI is working with.

post.description: Clean up and dispose the current LiteAI instance, releasing all resources.

get.description: Retrieve version control system (VCS) information for the current project, such as git branch.
Get a list of all available commands in the LiteAI system.
Get a list of all available AI agents in the LiteAI system.
Get a list of all available skills in the LiteAI system.
Get LSP server status
Get formatter status
Get events

start.description: Startup script to run when creating a new workspace (worktree)

custom.description: Allow typing a custom answer (default: true)

status.description: Current status of the task: pending, in_progress, completed, cancelled

priority.description: Priority level of the task: high, medium, low

ServerConfig.description: Server configuration for liteai serve and web commands

mdnsDomain.description: Custom domain name for mDNS service (default: liteai.local)

cors.description: Additional domains to allow for CORS

variant.description: Default model variant for this agent (applies only when using the agent's configured model).

description.description: Description of when to use the agent

hidden.description: Hide this subagent from the @ autocomplete menu (default: false, only applies to mode: subagent)

color.description: Color for the agent: hex code (e.g., #FF5733), theme color (primary, warning), or CSS color name (yellow, red)

steps.description: Maximum number of agentic iterations before forcing text-only response

tools.description: Allowed tools (Claude Code compat). Auto-mapped to permission allow rules.

disallowedTools.description: Denied tools (Claude Code compat). Auto-mapped to permission deny rules.

permissionMode.description: Permission preset (Claude Code compat). Mapped to LiteAI permission rules.

maxTurns.description: Maximum turns (Claude Code compat). Alias for steps.

skills.description: Preloaded skills (Claude Code compat). Injected on agent init.

background.description: Run concurrently (Claude Code compat). Requires background task infrastructure.

hooks.description: Per-agent hooks (Claude Code compat). Parsed but activates after hooks system.

disabled.description: Disable this variant for the model

enterpriseUrl.description: GitHub Enterprise URL for copilot authentication

setCacheKey.description: Enable promptCacheKey for this provider (default false)

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for requests to this provider. Default is 300000 (5 minutes). Set to false to disable timeout.

anyOf.description: Timeout in milliseconds for requests to this provider. Default is 300000 (5 minutes). Set to false to disable timeout.
Disable timeout for this provider entirely.

chunkTimeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds between streamed SSE chunks for this provider. If no chunk arrives within this window, the request is aborted.

command.description: Command and arguments to run the MCP server

environment.description: Environment variables to set when running the MCP server

enabled.description: Enable or disable the MCP server on startup

timeout.description: Timeout in ms for MCP server requests. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds) if not specified.

clientId.description: OAuth client ID. If not provided, dynamic client registration (RFC 7591) will be attempted.

clientSecret.description: OAuth client secret (if required by the authorization server)

scope.description: OAuth scopes to request during authorization

url.description: URL of the remote MCP server

enabled.description: Enable or disable the MCP server on startup

headers.description: Headers to send with the request

oauth.description: OAuth authentication configuration for the MCP server. Set to false to disable OAuth auto-detection.

timeout.description: Timeout in ms for MCP server requests. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds) if not specified.

allowedEnvVars.description: Env vars allowed for expansion in headers

$schema.description: JSON schema reference for configuration validation

urls.description: URLs to fetch skills from (e.g., https://example.com/.well-known/skills/)

share.description: Control sharing behavior:'manual' allows manual sharing via commands, 'auto' enables automatic sharing, 'disabled' disables all sharing

autoupdate.description: Automatically update to the latest version. Set to true to auto-update, false to disable, or 'notify' to show update notifications

disabled_providers.description: Disable providers that are loaded automatically

enabled_providers.description: When set, ONLY these providers will be enabled. All other providers will be ignored

model.description: Model to use in the format of provider/model, eg anthropic/claude-2

small_model.description: Small model to use for tasks like title generation in the format of provider/model

default_agent.description: Default agent to use when none is specified. Must be a primary agent. Falls back to 'build' if not set or if the specified agent is invalid.

username.description: Custom username to display in conversations instead of system username

provider.description: Custom provider configurations and model overrides

instructions.description: Additional instruction files or patterns to include

auto.description: Enable automatic compaction when context is full (default: true)

prune.description: Enable pruning of old tool outputs (default: true)

reserved.description: Token buffer for compaction. Leaves enough window to avoid overflow during compaction.

primary_tools.description: Tools that should only be available to primary agents.

continue_loop_on_deny.description: Continue the agent loop when a tool call is denied

mcp_timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for model context protocol (MCP) requests

hooks.description: Config-driven hooks that fire on lifecycle events (Claude Code compatible)

matcher.description: Regex pattern to filter when this hook group fires

enabledPlugins.description: Installed plugins and their enabled/disabled state (plugin-id → boolean)

extraKnownMarketplaces.description: Team-shared plugin marketplaces (name → source). Added marketplaces become available for plugin install.

telemetry.description: Telemetry settings. Telemetry is enabled by default — set disabled:true to opt out.

disabled.description: Set to true to opt out of all telemetry (traces, metrics, logs). Telemetry is enabled by default. Can also be controlled via the LITEAI_TELEMETRY_DISABLED env var.

startCommand.description: Additional startup script to run after the project's start command
